response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is primarily a full-stack TypeScript/Node.js engineer whose C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and isolated side projects — nowhere near the 5–10 years of production Windows-platform C++ the role demands. He has no demonstrable fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge; his SQL usage appears incidental rather than deep; and there is no evidence of Windows-platform specialisation, SQL Server/Oracle proficiency, or capital-markets tooling such as Summit or finance-grade messaging frameworks.
strengths: Polyglot background (C++, Java, DLang, TypeScript) demonstrates genuine language adaptability
Consistent CI/CD and DevOps practice (GitLab CI, Docker, Artifactory) satisfies one optional criterion
Strong public communication record via conference talks and multi-day workshops
concerns: C++ professional experience is limited to ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015; all subsequent roles use TypeScript, Node.js, or DLang — no evidence of sustained production C++ on any platform
No capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or investment banking domain knowledge; RIDE Capital is retail fintech, not the required domain
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle depth, no Windows-platform development history, and no mention of financial-systems tooling (Summit, Kafka/MQ in finance contexts)
